page: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ECACCV5 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 20 virtual CPUs mapped to 10 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and SVM virtualization flags. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable for external block storage attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the EC20as_cc_v5 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2702.38 in stress-ng single-core tests, which places it in the top 25% of tested instances. Memory performance is also a notable strength, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25% at 2819.58. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 27006.61 and large-block read bandwidth reaching 78.98 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 326.34 tokens per second with a small 135M LLM model. However, a significant performance tradeoff is observed in large-scale language model processing, where prompt processing for a 70B model drops to a poor 4.16 tokens per second, and text generation data is unavailable due to hardware limitations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the EC20as_cc_v5 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute core allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The absence of bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses reduces baseline resource overhead, allowing users to allocate budget strictly toward memory capacity and dedicated AMD EPYC compute. This resource balance is suited for relational databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, caching layers, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 20 vCPUs, 10 physical cores, and 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance and a top-tier Passmark memory score, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The instance excels at small-model LLM text generation but exhibits poor performance on large 70B models. Qualitatively, its high memory density relative to compute cores provides cost-efficient resource allocation for memory-bound tasks. It is best suited for databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory without local storage dependencies.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 server with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 CPU. It delivers strong single-core and memory performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC20as_cc_v5 features 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance featuring high memory density and strong single-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 20 vCPUs and 10 physical cores.
160.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Strong single-core CPU and Passmark memory benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU, optimized for external Premium Storage.
Suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications.
